hcd_data.architectural_style: Converted House-form, Semi-detached (confirmed – HCD Plan Vol. 2)
hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: The property has value as a House-form building that has been modified to accommodate a commercial conversion. Through its construction date and typology, the property is associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. With its visibly layered built form, the property is also associated with the patterns of commercial adaptation and expansion that began in the early twentieth century. The property has evolved over time while retaining the legibility of its original form and certain building features including roofline with side gable and partial front gable. The property has contextual value because it supports and maintains the historic mixed use character of the area. With its visible layering of residential and commercial built forms, the property is physically, functionally, visually, and historically linked to the surrounding market area.

context.street_character: One-way WB | 30 km/h | Sub-standard sidewalks & roadway | E-W commercial connector

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 4 storeys and is capped by a gable, consistent with its converted house-form, semi-detached typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 1, 1, 0, 0 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Character-defining facade elements include decorative brick, cornice.
facade_detail.heritage_summary: With its visible layering of residential and commercial built forms, the property is physically, functionally, visually, and historically linked to the surrounding market area. The property has value as a House-form building that has been modified to accommodate a commercial conversion.

facade_description: Four-storey mixed-material facade with a gable roof, approximately 5.2 m wide. Photo observation shows a white-painted upper facade with a pale blue Coral Sea awning over a dark storefront opening at grade. A projecting balcony at the upper level and oversized signboard substantially alter the original pre-1889 house-form. Only limited front glazing is visible because signage and balcony elements obscure much of the elevation. The side gable and partial front gable roofline survive from the original construction. The facade is visually cluttered with graffiti on side walls, later balcony railings, and commercial signage, though the layered residential-over-commercial form remains legible.
heritage_summary: With its visible layering of residential and commercial built forms, the property is physically, functionally, visually, and historically linked to the surrounding market area. The property has value as a House-form building that has been modified to accommodate a commercial conversion.
